description: The Teams Toolkit now uses the exact Valley Creek green of the Circle Leader Toolkit and shows the Valley Creek logo in the sign-in hero and header, for one consistent brand across both leader toolkits.

The Teams Toolkit now hides the RADIUS app's bottom navigation, footer, and quick-actions button, so team leaders get the same clean, full-screen toolkit experience as the Circle Leader Toolkit — on both the main domain and the dedicated teams site.

A team leader's profile now shows the full Teams Toolkit admin section — open their toolkit, text them their sign-in link, toggle leader access, and toggle email reminders — matching the Circle Leader Toolkit controls on circle leader profiles.

Added a Schedule tab to the Teams Toolkit. Team leaders can see, by upcoming date, who's scheduled in their positions and each person's response — accepted, declined (with reason), or pending — and tap to call or text them. Scheduling is still managed in CCB; this is a read-only view.

From a team leader's profile you can now tap 'Open Teams Toolkit' to jump into their toolkit with an auto sign-in, or 'Text Teams Toolkit link' to send them their personal sign-in link — the same one-tap access circle leaders already have.
Hardened the dedicated Teams Toolkit site so it can never double-run scheduled jobs (reminder emails, push, CCB syncs) even if a config flag is missed — those run on the main RADIUS site only.

Launched the Teams Toolkit — a dedicated, passwordless hub for team leaders that mirrors the Circle Leader Toolkit. Team leaders sign in with an emailed code and see their team roster (the volunteers in the positions they lead) plus an inbox and message center. Schedules with RSVP are coming next.

When configuring a host team leader's positions, you can now find the CCB scheduling category by typing its name (e.g. "Host Teams") instead of needing the numeric ID — with a searchable dropdown showing the category name, campus, and ID. Entering an ID directly still works.

You can now remove a managed position from a host team leader after assigning it. Open Edit Positions to see a "Currently managed" list — now labeled with the CCB category name — with a Remove button on each, or look up the category and uncheck positions (your current selections are pre-checked).
Fixed the Serve Team Roster showing empty even after positions were configured. Newly saved positions and category IDs now appear immediately instead of being masked by a stale server cache.

You can now configure a host team leader's CCB positions directly from their Roster tab — no need to re-import. Enter the CCB scheduling category ID, select which positions they manage, and save. An Edit Positions button lets you update the selection anytime.

Serve Team Roster for host team leaders — when importing a team, select which CCB positions this leader manages (e.g., Parking, Greeters). Their leader profile now includes a Roster tab showing every volunteer in those positions, pulled live from CCB, with name, phone, email, and quick-action buttons.

New Import Host Team page — enter a CCB scheduling category ID to look up any host team, preview its campus, positions, and volunteer count, designate the team leader via CCB person search, and create a RADIUS profile for them. Accessible from the admin nav under Import Host Team.

Editing a circle is now cleaner and tied to CCB by IDs. In edit mode there's a 'Re-sync from CCB' button that re-pulls the meeting time/day/frequency/location, leader name/email/phone/birthday, group name, and calendar events — and now shows you a before→after list of exactly what will change and asks you to confirm before saving. The long CCB link fields are hidden while editing — instead you set the CCB Group ID, Individual ID, and Event IDs directly, and RADIUS builds the links for you. The import page now requires a director (ACPD) to be selected before a circle can be imported.

You can now set a Circle Leader's status to Archived and have it save. The database was rejecting the Archived status even though it appeared in the dropdown. Save errors on the leader profile now also show the actual reason instead of a generic message.

Newly added Circles and Leaders now show up in the global search (⌘K) right away. The search index used to load once and go stale until a full page refresh — it now refreshes each time you open the search.

Import a circle from CCB by entering its Group ID. RADIUS pulls the circle's details and the leader's name, email, phone, and birthday so you can review before importing. Meeting day, exact meeting time, frequency (weekly/bi-weekly), and location are read straight from the circle's CCB calendar event — more accurate than CCB's coarse group labels. Assign a director (ACPD), then import it as a new circle in one click. The CCB calendar events are linked automatically so event-summary syncing works right away.

Added an 'Event Summary Follow-up' connection type for logging on a leader's page, plus a Touchpoint Type multi-select filter on the Touchpoint Tracker. Pick which types count toward coverage — all of them, or just certain ones like Event Summary Follow-up.

New Touchpoint Tracker — see at a glance which Circle Leaders you've touched base with about their event summaries this period, and which are still overdue. Filter by campus, ACPD, or status, sort any column, and view coverage rollups across every ACPD and campus. Connections logged on a leader's page count too, and the last-touchpoint date links straight to that leader's notes. Replaces tracking this in a spreadsheet.

Added a Log Touchpoint button on each event summary in the Event Explorer. Log that you followed up on a leader's debrief — and text or call them right from the same modal. Logged touchpoints also show up in the leader's notes and coaching timeline.

Added a Touchpoint Cadence setting — one central target (e.g. 1 per leader) that applies to every campus, plus savable date-range periods (define Spring, Summer, Fall, or any ranges in advance). The tracker uses whichever period contains today.

Smoothed out the Circles Toolkit home-screen app launch on iPhone. The blank white screen is gone — opening the app now goes straight to the branded green splash with the Circles logo, which holds for a brief, purposeful beat (no more flash) before settling into the page.

Temporarily hid the Health section of the Circle Leader Toolkit while it's being finalized. The Health tab no longer appears, and the page isn't reachable yet — it'll return once it's ready to roll out.
Fixed the Circle Leader Toolkit home-screen app sending leaders to the RADIUS login. Adding the Toolkit to your iPhone home screen now opens the Toolkit and keeps you signed in — no logging back in.

New Get Started page — a guided, ten-stop walkthrough of the tools you’ll use most, from Events to the circle profile to the Circle List, Boards, Today, and Notebook. Starts with one-time setup to install Radius on your phone or desktop and turn on alerts, and covers onboarding leaders into the Circle Leader Toolkit. Track your progress as you go, and find it anytime under your avatar menu.
Team messaging is taking a short break — we’ve temporarily hidden it from the app while we keep refining it. Your Inbox (notifications) is unaffected and still on.

Fixed a “Not a member of this conversation” error that could block deleting a conversation you’re clearly in. The membership check is now tolerant of duplicate records, and a cleanup removes any duplicates that had crept in.

Name a group thread: open a group’s options menu and choose “Rename group” to give it a clear title (leave it blank to fall back to members’ names). Auto-generated group names are now shorter and no longer stretch the screen.

Deleting a conversation or message (and editing a message) now works reliably — these actions were using HTTP methods the host can block, so they now go through a method that always reaches the server.

Fixed starting a new conversation getting stuck on a spinner (the thread now loads its own details instead of waiting for the list), made deleting a conversation reliably remove it, and surfaced messaging errors on screen instead of failing silently.

More control in threads: edit your own messages (they show an “edited” tag), pin an important message to the top of a conversation, and mute a conversation to stop its push notifications (an @mention still reaches you). Find these in a message’s actions or the conversation menu.
Read receipts: your last sent message now shows “Seen” once the other person reads it (or “Seen by N” in a group), so you know it landed.
@mention a director in a thread to ping them directly — they get a dedicated inbox notification and push, even if they’ve muted the conversation. Mentions are highlighted in the message.
Search is here: find any past message across your conversations from the Messages search box, and filter your Inbox by typing in its new search field.
Start a group message: in New Message, pick several directors to create a group thread (one person still starts a direct message). Group threads get their own icon and are named from the members.
Like a message with a 💚 — tap the heart on any message to react, and tap again to remove it. Like counts show on the message and update live.
Delete messages and conversations: remove your own messages from a thread, or delete an entire direct or group conversation from its options menu. (The shared ACPD Team channel can't be deleted.)

Polished the new Messages and Inbox screens: conversations now stretch edge-to-edge (no gap on the right), starting a direct message no longer shows a blank screen while it opens, and the notification settings toggles render as proper switches instead of tall boxes.
On mobile, the More tab now shows a red dot when you have unread inbox notifications or team messages waiting inside it — so you can tell at a glance without opening the menu.
Messages and the Inbox now open full-screen for a focused, app-like experience — the surrounding navigation steps out of the way, with a back button to return to the rest of Radius.

Filtering the Inbox by type is now a drawer with checkboxes, so you can combine types — e.g. see card assignments and comments together — instead of picking just one. The Filter button shows how many types are active.

You can now forward a message to another conversation. Hover (or tap) a message and choose Forward, then pick the team channel, an existing DM, or start a new one with another director — the forwarded note carries who and where it came from.

Inbox notifications now reach your phone. Card assignments, comments, board and notebook shares, and daily birthday and follow-up alerts send a push notification (in addition to the in-app badge) so you don’t have to be in Radius to know something needs you. Respects your notification settings.

Added your Inbox — one place for everything that needs your attention. It collects team messages, card assignments, card comments, board and notebook shares, and daily birthday and follow-up alerts for your leaders. Filter by type, mark read or unread, archive, or delete, and use Notification settings to choose exactly which kinds of activity show up. Open it from the new bell icon in the top bar (or under Activity in the mobile menu); the badge shows your unread count.

Added Messages — a built-in way for the ACPD team to talk to each other inside Radius. Everyone shares a team channel, and you can start a private one-on-one with any other director. New messages show an unread badge in the nav and, if you turn on notifications, a push alert on your phone even when Radius is closed. Find it on the new chat icon in the top bar (or under Team in the mobile menu).

Added a little more breathing room at the bottom of the mobile navigation bar so the corner tabs like "Today" and "More" are easier to reach. The bar now sits a bit higher off the bottom edge of the screen.

Fixed the bottom navigation bar occasionally floating up into the middle of the page while scrolling on mobile. The bar is now pinned to its own layer so it stays anchored at the bottom of the screen. Also removed the redundant green dot on the More tab — the red alert dot already lets you know when something needs attention.

Fixed the cramped day-view header on Today so the full weekday and date are always visible. The date used to get squeezed onto the same row as the day arrows, Today button, reminders, and Calendars button and would cut off to something like "Thursday…". The date now sits on its own line above the controls, so you can always read the whole day and date at a glance.

Added a red alert dot next to Today in the navigation whenever you have open items — board cards and follow-ups due today or overdue. It's the same count that drives the app-icon badge, so you can see at a glance that something needs attention without opening the page, and it ticks away in real time as you clear items off your Today list. The dot shows on both the desktop top bar and the mobile tab bar.

Rounded out the Coaching Automations hub. You can now Preview exactly what the next run would send (without delivering anything) or Run now to send due nudges immediately, right from the Org Defaults tab. The Messages tab gained a live Preview that renders each message with sample data as you edit. The Leaders tab now hides archived leaders and flags anyone without a linked Circle group so the list reflects who can actually receive nudges. Per-leader coaching settings now live only in the hub's Leaders tab (the separate tab on the Circle page was removed to avoid two places doing the same thing). Behind the scenes the daily run was reworked to batch its lookups so it stays fast as the number of leaders grows.

Turned Coaching Automations into a full management hub, now in the main Tools menu. A new Leaders tab lists every leader with their current coaching settings, filterable by campus, circle type, and ACPD — select any group to mass-apply settings or reset them to the org defaults, or expand a single leader to fine-tune them right there. A new Messages tab lets you edit the wording of every coaching nudge (with placeholders like the leader's first name and roster count) and reset any message back to the default. The org-wide defaults live on their own tab in the same place.

Gave per-leader coaching automation settings their own roomy page. There's now a 'Coaching' tab in the Circle Leader section navigation (admins only) where you can customize a leader's thresholds or reset them to the org defaults — replacing the cramped panel that used to sit on the profile. On the org-wide Coaching Automations admin page, a new 'Apply defaults to all leaders' button clears every leader's custom settings so they all follow the defaults, with a clear confirmation warning before it overwrites anything.

Circle Leaders now receive timely, life-giving coaching nudges right in their Toolkit inbox. The app watches for real moments worth acting on — a Circle growing large enough to dream about multiplication, a brand-new member to welcome within a day, someone who hasn't attended in weeks, a birthday that week, a Circle that hasn't met for a stretch, and first-time guests to follow up with. Each nudge is warmly worded and suggests a next step. ACPD admins set the org-wide thresholds on the new Coaching Automations settings page, and can fine-tune or turn off any automation for an individual leader right from their Circle page.

Fixed the Toolkit header so long Circle names always fit. The big '[Name]'s Circle' title now shrinks just enough to stay on one line within the header instead of running off the right edge, while shorter names keep the full large size. It re-fits automatically when the screen size changes.

Fixed the Project Boards header on mobile. The Boards / Calendar / Forms tabs and the New Board button were running off the right edge of the screen and getting cut off. On phones they now stack neatly: the tabs share a full-width row and the New Board button sits directly below them, so everything stays tappable and on-screen.

Made due dates easier to find on board cards, especially on mobile. Inside a card, the Start Date and Due Date (with time) now sit at the top of the details panel above Priority, so the most time-sensitive info is the first thing you see instead of a scroll away. On the board cards themselves, the date and time now get their own row with the Snooze button placed directly beneath them, so a card's schedule reads as one clear unit.

Added a Messaging page for each Circle Leader, reached from a new Messaging tab or the Message quick action on their profile. Send a message straight to the leader's Toolkit Inbox, schedule messages for later (and edit, send-now, or cancel them), and quick-send from saved templates that auto-fill the leader's first name. A message history timeline shows every message sent to the leader in order, with read receipts, timestamps, and paging back through older messages. All activity is tied specifically to that leader. The leader profile's section navigation is now a compact dropdown so it stays tidy as sections are added.

Made the "Did Not Meet" insights trustworthy. The dashboard now categorizes reasons straight from the actual choices leaders pick — Holiday weekend and Weather count as valid, while Low attendance and Leader out of town surface as coaching opportunities, and any free-text explanation is listed on its own to read. Previously the dashboard guessed categories by keyword matching, which quietly mislabeled common reasons (for example, "Leader out of town" was dropped into "Other").

Reworked how dates work on the Circle Reporting dashboard. Instead of a preset dropdown, you now set a start and end date that stick between visits. The end date has a "Through end of last week" button that pins it to the most recently completed Saturday and rolls forward on its own each week, so the report always reflects finished weeks without you touching it. Quick buttons for Last 4 weeks, Last 12 weeks, Semester to date, and Year to date are still there, and your campus, type, and status filters are remembered too.

Added an export for every event summary in the selected date range, built for handing to AI for analysis. "Export JSON (AI)" produces a structured file with the report's filters, headline totals, and each circle's full topic, notes, prayer requests, attendance, and did-not-meet reason. "Export CSV" gives the same data as a spreadsheet. Both respect the campus, type, and status filters you have applied.

Board list automations gained new rules. You can now set a due date a number of days after a card arrives (e.g. "due 3 days after it lands here") instead of only a fixed calendar date, and you can move a card automatically the moment it's assigned, focused, or tagged with a specific label — great for lanes like "Up Next" or routing anything tagged Blocked to a Needs Help list.

Reworked the list automations panel so it's clearer. Each rule now reads as a plain sentence ("When a card arrives → Set priority to high"), rules are grouped by what triggers them, and your changes save automatically — no more adding a rule and losing it by closing the window. Selected labels now highlight in solid green so it's obvious what you've picked.

Board lists can now move cards automatically when a due date is added. Open a list's automations and add "Move when due date set" with a destination list — from then on, any card in that list that receives a due date (via the card, the quick date picker, or list actions) slides over to the list you picked. Great for a "Scheduled" or "Up Next" lane that fills itself as you set dates.

Fixed the "Open Toolkit" button on a Circle Leader's profile so it reliably signs you straight into that leader's toolkit instead of landing on the sign-in screen. The auto-login link now stays on the RADIUS site that issued it, so it no longer depends on the dedicated toolkit site sharing a matching sign-in secret. Links you text to leaders still use the clean circlestoolkit.netlify.app address.

You can now schedule when a Leader Message lands in Circle Leaders' Toolkit inboxes. When composing, choose "Schedule for later" and pick a date and time — the message stays hidden until then, when it drops into inboxes and fires its push automatically. Scheduled messages show a "scheduled" badge with their delivery time, and you can edit them, change the time or recipients, or hit "Send now" to deliver early. All times are church (Central) time.

The Message Center, Leader Messages, and Circle Leader Resources editors are now true WYSIWYG. As you type, the editor itself matches the leader-facing look (white card, Open Sans, green links and buttons), and a new live "Preview — what leaders see" pane below renders the exact toolkit card, including YouTube embeds and styled call-to-action buttons. No more guessing how a message will look once it reaches leaders.

The app-icon badge now clears correctly when you finish a card that's due today. Previously, completed cards due today still counted toward the badge, so the number on the home-screen icon could stay stuck even after everything was done. Cleared follow-ups now drop out of the count too.

The Circle Leader Toolkit now has its own home at circlestoolkit.netlify.app — a clean, dedicated site for leaders with a tidy address bar (no more long /circle-leader-toolkit paths). Reminder emails and notifications point there too. It still opens from the main app, so nothing you've bookmarked breaks.

Fixed adding a card from the Today timeline on mobile. Tapping an empty slot now opens the quick-add dialog near the top of the screen so the keyboard no longer hides the Add card button, and the dialog no longer closes from a stray tap before your card is saved.

Start the day with a morning digest. Around 7 AM, devices with reminders on get one notification summarizing your plate — cards due today, anything overdue, follow-ups, and your first timed item — and it refreshes the app-icon badge for the day. Quiet when you have nothing open.

The RADIUS app icon now shows a badge with how many cards and follow-ups are still open today (including overdue). Reminder notifications update the badge even while the app is closed, and it ticks down live as you complete items with the app open. Works on installed RADIUS apps — home screen on iPhone, dock or taskbar on desktop.

Reminders now reach your phone even when the app is closed. Turning on the bell on the Today timeline registers this device for push notifications — about 10 minutes before each card or follow-up with a time, a notification arrives, and tapping it opens your Today page. Works on any device where you've installed RADIUS; each device you enable gets its own reminders.

Plan your day right on the timeline. Drag any card or follow-up — from the all-day strip, your Big 3, or your focus cards — onto an hour to schedule it there, or drag a scheduled block to a new hour to move it. Click an empty slot to create a new card due at that time. Everything you place on the field locks to a one-hour block.

Flip through days on the Today timeline. Arrows page to tomorrow or back to yesterday — handy for setting up the next morning before you leave — and a Today button snaps you back. Scheduling and quick-add work on whichever day you're viewing, and your subscribed calendars come along. The list on the left always stays on today.
A Next Up strip above the timeline shows what's coming next and how long until it starts. Turn on the bell to get a notification 10 minutes before each timed item while the app is open.
Calendar events on the timeline are now clickable — a quick popover shows the event's time, which calendar it's from, the location, and any notes.

The Today page now uses the full screen on desktop. Your Big 3, focus cards, and all of today's items live in a rail on the left with the scoreboard pinned at the bottom, while the right two-thirds shows your day chronologically — all-day items up top and timed items laid out hour by hour from midnight to midnight, with the 8:30–5:30 workday highlighted and a live now-line. Filter chips let you choose which kinds of items appear on the timeline. Clicking any card opens the full card editor right there, no trip to the board needed.

Subscribe to your external calendars on the Today page. Paste a calendar's secret iCal address (Google, Outlook, or Apple) under the Calendars button and its events appear on your day timeline in the color you pick. Calendars can be toggled on and off or removed at any time.
On your phone, the Today page now has a List / Day toggle. List keeps the familiar stacked view; Day shows the new hour-by-hour timeline — including your subscribed calendars — sized for one hand.

The Circle Leader Toolkit can now be reached at its own dedicated web address, separate from the Radius admin app, with no Radius branding or admin pages visible. Sign-in links and reminder emails sent to leaders now point to that address. Leader-facing toolkit emails also send through their own dedicated email account, kept separate from Radius's internal admin emails.

Closed a database access gap on the Leader Messages stats view. The recipient/read/unread counts view ran with elevated privileges that bypassed row-level security, so it could have exposed message stats to roles that have no access to the underlying inbox tables. The view now enforces the same access rules as those tables, with client roles explicitly revoked. No change to how the page works — admin access flows through secure server routes as before.

Fixed already-reported event summaries showing as "Pending." The cause was Church Community Builder's daily API limit being used up, which left Radius unable to read attendance. The reminder system was the main culprit — it was calling CCB live for every leader every few minutes; it now reads from the once-a-day cache instead, cutting that traffic by ~95%. We also added a hard daily ceiling so automated jobs can never exhaust the CCB quota again, and the events page now shows a clear "can't reach CCB right now" note (and falls back to the last known status) instead of silently marking submitted summaries as Pending.

Added test buttons on the Settings page to verify push notifications and email are working. Click "Test push" or "Test email" to send yourself sample messages and confirm your notification preferences are set up correctly.

Renamed the leader-facing app from "Circle Leader Dashboard" to "Circle Leader Toolkit" everywhere it appears — the app title, install/home-screen name, Help & Guide header, notifications, and the link from a leader's profile. When you add it to your home screen it now installs as "Toolkit." The web address also moved to /circle-leader-toolkit — your existing links, bookmarks, and home-screen app keep working and forward to the new address automatically.
Moved Add Circle/Leader and Mass Update up into the Tools section of the menu so the most-used admin actions are easier to reach. They remain admin-only.

Deleting a user now actually removes their login account. Previously the app reported "successfully deleted" but the person's sign-in account stayed behind in the background, so they could still receive magic links. Deletion now removes both the profile and the login account, and reports a real error if the login account can't be removed.

Reorganized the Tools menu — Leadership Snapshots and Health Questions now live under the renamed "Circle Leader Toolkit" section, grouping the leader-facing tools together instead of splitting them between sections.

Simplified Circle Summary reminder emails. Instead of a reminder before your Circle plus repeated morning nudges afterward, you now get a single email about 30 minutes after your Circle starts, reminding you to tell us how it went. One tap signs you in and takes you straight to your summary.
Fixed Circle Summary emails — sign-in codes and meeting reminders — that had stopped being delivered. They are now sent from a verified address again so they reach your inbox.
Removed the standalone Leadership Snapshot tool from the Tools menu. Leadership health snapshots still live on each circle leader's profile and in the admin Leadership Snapshots view.

Added a contact button to the birthday reminders on your roster. When someone's birthday is coming up, tap the phone icon on the reminder to instantly call or text them a happy birthday — no need to scroll down and find them in the list.
Added a "Report a bug" link to the footer of the Circle Leader Dashboard so leaders can quickly flag anything that isn't working right.

Introduced the Leadership Snapshot — a self-assessment Circle Leaders take from the new Health tab in their Circle Leader Dashboard to reflect on five areas of leadership health: Personal Spiritual Health, Circle Leadership, Discipleship & Multiplication, Community & Relationships, and Alignment & Support. Each submission is scored by category and paired with an AI-generated summary plus next steps for every area, and leaders can revisit past results and reassess anytime. On the staff side you can review every submission, drill into each category, watch a leader's scores trend over time, edit one entered incorrectly, and revert to any previous version. Staff can also take it from /leadership-snapshot in RADIUS.

Added public intake Forms for boards. Build a form, map its fields to a card's title, description, priority, due date, and assignee, then share a public link (/f/your-form) that anyone can fill out without logging in. Each submission automatically creates a card in the column you choose, and you can review every submission, jump to the card it created, search them, and export to CSV.

Made haptic feedback noticeably stronger so taps actually register on supported phones, and added a "Test buzz" button under Settings → App Management → Haptic Feedback to confirm haptics work on your device. Note: iOS only supports web haptics in Safari (not installed home-screen apps).
Updated the profile page header banner to green and removed the Radius AI Assistant toggle.

Added haptic feedback throughout the app — buttons, nav, and toggles give a light tap, and completing a checklist item gives a stronger confirmation buzz. Works on most Android phones and newer iPhones (iOS 17.4+); it's a no-op on desktop. You can turn it off anytime under Settings → App Management → Haptic Feedback.

Added an A–Z quick-jump rail down the right edge of the Prayer page — tap or drag a letter to jump straight to that leader. Each leader now also has a phone button that opens a Call / Text sheet using their number on file (including the co-leader's number when present).
Refreshed the loading screen: the Circles logo now sits centered with rings gently pulsing outward from it, replacing the small spinner for a calmer, more branded feel.

Setting a Circle Leader's status to Archived now saves correctly. Previously the database rejected the Archived value, so the change silently failed with a generic error. Archived leaders also now show a muted gray status badge.

Opening the dashboard now holds a steady green loading screen until your circle is ready — no more flash of a black or white screen between loads. The status bar and browser chrome stay green to match, and the app reaches your events in fewer redirects.
The "Circle Leader Dashboard" button on a leader's profile now shows the white Circles logo instead of the generic open-link icon, matching the app's branding.
Renamed the leader app (formerly "Circle Leader Hub") and the admin app to "Circle Leader Dashboard." When you save it to your home screen, it now installs simply as "Dashboard."

Added a Help & Guide page to the Circle Leader Dashboard with friendly, step-by-step instructions for everything in the app — submitting summaries, managing your roster, the inbox, resources, and notifications — plus a short why behind each one. You can reach it from a link in the Events footer and from the Settings page.

When sending a Leader Message, the recipient preview now shows who will actually get a push notification versus who will only see it in their Hub inbox. Each recipient gets a "Push" or "In-app only" badge, a summary tells you how many of each, you can filter the list, and a one-tap "Nudge to enable push" button re-prompts the in-app-only leaders to turn notifications on next time they open the Hub.

Cleaned up the contact lines on the Circle roster: the birthday icon is now a clear birthday-cake glyph instead of looking like a briefcase, and the phone, email, and birthday icons all share the same green so the envelope no longer looks oddly greyed out.
The global search box now has breathing room around your text and a softer rounded corner, so the field no longer feels crowded when you start typing.

Cards you complete from the Today page now land in the right place automatically. When you open a board, RADIUS checks its list rules and sweeps any already-completed cards into their "move when completed" destination (like Done) — no more marking cards incomplete and complete again just to get them to move.

You can now snooze the "hasn't attended" alerts on your Circle roster. Tap Snooze on any alert to quiet it for 7 days, or use "Snooze all · 7 days" to clear the whole stack at once — the alert comes back if the person still hasn't shown up. The alert cards were also tidied up for mobile, and the header Settings gear no longer crowds the Circle name on smaller screens.

Added an "Add Circle Roster" option to Bulk Message. Search for a circle and add everyone on its current CCB roster as recipients in one click — no more adding members one at a time. Roster members are tagged so you can see which circle they came from, and anyone without a phone number is skipped automatically.

Cleaned up the navigation badges so a "0" no longer shows on the Events or Inbox tabs. A count now appears only when you actually have something waiting — a summary to submit or an unread message — and disappears once you're caught up.

Gave the header Settings button room to breathe on phones. It now shows as a compact gear icon on mobile so it no longer overlaps your Circle title, and expands back to the full "Settings" label on larger screens.

Refreshed the Notification Settings page: the preference toggles now use proper switches instead of plain checkboxes, and the page reflects reality — when push is already on for your browser it shows a "Push on" status with a disable option, instead of always offering "Enable push." Browser permission state (Allowed, Blocked, Not set) and your enabled devices are now shown as clear status badges.

Moved Settings out of the tab bar and into a gear button in the header, freeing up room for the Events, Roster, Resources, and Inbox tabs. Sign out now lives at the bottom of the Settings page instead of floating in the header.

Fixed a rare timezone edge case where a Circle meeting held late on the last night of the week could be matched to the wrong week — making its summary appear missing. Week matching now uses Central time consistently across the tracker list, review modal, and bulk review.

Fixed event summaries showing "No notes recorded" even when a leader wrote a full write-up. The leader's narrative is captured through the summary form's questions (e.g. "Tell us about your Circle gathering"), and the review modal wasn't surfacing those answers — only the legacy notes field. The modal now displays the complete write-up exactly as it appears in CCB.

Added a "Remove from roster" button right on the absent-member alerts in your Roster tab. It opens a quick confirmation that encourages keeping your roster focused on who's actually showing up — and reminds you that anyone can be added back anytime if they return to the Circle.

Smoothed out the Circle Summary loading experience. Signing in now goes straight to your events with one solid loading screen instead of a chain of loaders flashing in sequence. Once you're in, switching tabs shows a lightweight skeleton that keeps the header and tabs on screen — no more full splash on every tap.
Added optional time field to follow-up modals so you can schedule follow-ups at a specific time of day.

title: Editing a Circle Summary keeps your attendance
description: Fixed a bug where editing and resubmitting a Circle Summary reset everyone you'd marked present back to zero in CCB. Resubmitting now keeps your full attendance list intact, so updating your notes or Circle details no longer wipes out who attended.

title: Snappier Circle Summary navigation
description: Moving around your Circle now feels instant. Switching between the Events and Roster tabs no longer throws up a full-screen loading splash on every tap — your header and tabs stay put and the content swaps with a light placeholder. And tapping Cancel on a summary returns you straight to your events list instead of reloading it.

title: Faster Circle Summary loads
description: Your Circle pages now load noticeably faster. The events and roster tabs render with your data already in place instead of flashing a spinner first, the header name appears instantly, and behind the scenes we cut several redundant lookups on every page so things feel snappier — without changing any of your data.

Logging a prayer for a circle leader is now a single tap — the "Pray" button records it and gets out of your way, no longer popping open a note box. Adding a note is now its own button (the pencil next to "Pray") for the times you want to jot something down.

When you mark a circle leader's prayer as answered, RADIUS now asks if you'd like to save it as a note on that leader's profile. The note text is pre-filled and editable, so you can capture the answered prayer in their history with one tap — or skip it if you'd rather not.
Each meeting in your Circle's Events list now shows how many people attended right next to "Summary on file," so you can see turnout at a glance without opening each meeting.

Your Circle roster now loads attendance info instantly. Previously the names appeared first and then the "Last attended" tags and the absent-member alerts popped in a moment later. Those values are now remembered between visits and shown right away, with a brief placeholder on the very first load so the list no longer jumps around.

Cleaned up the circle profile's Circle Info section: the CCB Group Name Override now only appears when it's actually set, so empty fields no longer clutter the page. When editing, the field is clearly marked optional and includes a plain-English note explaining that it's only needed when a circle is saved under a different name in CCB than the leader's.
The Big 3 section on the Today page now sorts your priorities by due date, with the soonest-due card at the top and any open slots at the bottom, so the most urgent work is always first.
The RADIUS logo in the top navigation now links to the Events page, and the quick-actions menu icons were recolored from blue to the Valley Creek brand green.

Polished modal styling: button labels are now centered in all branded buttons, primary action buttons (Save Follow-Up, Set Follow-Up, Add Note) now use the Valley Creek brand green instead of slate/orange, and the modal header bar was changed from navy to a neutral gray to match the rest of the app.

Fixed an annoying bug on card detail panels where highlighting text in the description or checklist by click-dragging would close the card if you released the mouse outside the panel. The card now only closes when you actually click the dimmed backdrop.

Toned down the circle profile sub-navigation bar (Profile / Notes / Scorecard / Care). The bright green gradient was replaced with a subtle dark gray that blends with the rest of the app, while the active tab keeps its green underline accent.
Added a Snooze button to board cards with a due date. One click pushes the due date to the next business day (Monday–Thursday), so you can quickly defer a card without opening the date picker.

Refreshed the app's accent color to Valley Creek green. Active nav links, the floating action buttons, page toggles/filter chips, the circle profile tab bar, loading spinners, and the splash screen now use the brand green instead of blue. Several navy-tinted page and card backgrounds (Event Summary, Progress, Message Center, Leader Messages, Resources, circle profile cards) were also neutralized to a darker gray to match the rest of the app.

Added a 'View in CCB' button to the event summary review modal. It opens the meeting's event detail page in CCB for that exact occurrence date, so you can jump straight to the source record while reviewing.

Circle Summary events list now goes back 12 weeks instead of 8, so leaders can see and back-fill summaries further into the semester. The roster page's 'last attended' badges now read from the same shared cache as the events list, so opening the roster no longer triggers a fresh CCB pull every visit.
Moved 'Find A Circle' (now renamed 'Circle List') into the main navigation, and moved 'Progress' into the Tools menu.

Simplified the Invite a Person modal — the separate template editor is gone. Toggle 'Show placeholders' on the Message Preview to swap between the rendered message and the template view (with clickable placeholder chips below). When your template differs from your saved default, a 'Save as default for future invites?' prompt appears inline.

Placeholder chips in the Invite a Person template editor are now clickable — click {personFirstName}, {location}, {day}, etc. to drop the token into your template at the cursor (or appended to the end if the editor isn't focused). The Message Preview above also updates live as you tweak the template, so you can see exactly what the prospective person will receive.
Reordered Find a Circle columns to: Circle Leader, Type, Day, Time, Location, Campus, Roster.
The Roster column on Find a Circle is now centered and sortable — click the header to sort by roster size (ascending or descending).

The Invite a Person flow (on Find a Circle and on the circle detail page) now uses a customizable message template with placeholders for {personName}, {acpdName}, {leaderName}, {day}, {time}, {frequency}, and {location}. Edit the default once, save it to your profile, and every future invite starts from your version. A new 'Invite a Person' button has also been added to the circle detail page under 'Connect New Person'.
Prayer points are now scoped to your own user — the Prayer List only shows prayer points and session logs you created, so they're no longer visible across accounts.

Find a Circle now shows a Location column, an ACPD filter for signed-in users, and defaults to sorting by day-of-week (Sunday → Saturday) instead of alphabetical day order. Edit a circle's location from the circle detail page.

If someone on your Circle roster hasn't attended in 15+ days, the roster page now shows a red banner at the top with quick Text and Call buttons so you can reach out right away. Dismiss the banner if you've already followed up — it'll come back automatically the next time they miss after attending again.

Every Circle Leader row on the Prayer page now has a small 'Pray' button so you can log that you prayed for a leader even when there are no specific prayer requests on file. Each tap records a session — with optional note — and shows last-prayed time and full history under the leader.

Each person on the Circle Leader roster now shows their last attended date, pulled from CCB attendance. If it's been more than 14 days since they attended, the row gets a red 'last attended … Xd ago' badge so leaders can spot members drifting away.

Circle Leader events list now flags 'Did Not Meet' submissions in red — red left border, light red row background, a red 'Did Not Meet' badge, and a semester-level count chip at the top — so leaders can see at a glance how many weeks they didn't meet.

Each circle row on Find A Circle now has an 'Invite' button. Click it to pull up a CCB person lookup, pre-fill a friendly text describing when and where the circle meets, and send it straight to Messages so the prospective member can confirm the schedule works.

Circles still in 'Awaiting Submission' more than 24 hours after their meeting now light up red with an overdue badge, a pulsing alert icon, and a prominent red Send Reminder button. The bucket header also shows a count of overdue circles, and overdue rows float to the top so you can chase them first.

You can now customize the default Connect New Person message per ACPD. Open the Connect New Person modal and click 'Customize default template' to edit your saved template with placeholders like {leaderFirstName}, {personName}, {phone}, and {email}.
Card descriptions in the notebook side panel now render as friendly text and clickable links instead of raw HTML. Click the description to edit it.

Closed an authentication gap where signed-out users could reach internal pages (like the Event Summary Tracker) directly by URL. All pages now require sign-in except the login page and the public Find a Circle search.

Checklist items on board cards can now be converted into their own cards. Click the arrow icon on any checklist item, choose a list, and hit Convert — the item becomes a new card and is removed from the checklist.
Message Center banners on the Circle Summary events page now render YouTube links as inline video embeds, matching how the Inbox and Resources pages already handle them.

Leader Messages admin page now scales smoothly: recipient/unread/read counts come from a single aggregated query instead of pulling every recipient row, target previews fetch only the leaders that match the chosen campus/ACPD/circle, and editing a sent message no longer rewrites every recipient row. The page stays fast as message history grows.

New Resources tab on the Circle Summary, plus a 'Circle Leader Resources' admin page under Tools. ACPDs can publish a rich-text page (links, lists, headings, and buttons) that every Circle Leader sees inside their Circle Summary.
Renamed the 'Meet' status segment on the Event Summary Tracker to 'Circle Met' for clarity.

Faster Circle Summary roster: the page now loads from a server-side roster cache instead of waiting on a live CCB call every time. Add/remove actions update the cache instantly so changes appear immediately, and the cache transparently re-syncs from CCB in the background after 15 minutes.

Circle Summary now installs as its own PWA with a dedicated icon: the white Circles logo centered on the page's signature green. Adding /circle-leader-toolkit to your home screen launches straight into the Circle Summary sign-in flow, not the main RADIUS app. Magic-link previews in iMessage now show 'Circle Summary' as the title instead of 'Signing you in…'.

Notebook opens faster: the app remembers your last-opened page and jumps to it instantly on reload, and the most-recent page lookup now fetches full content in one query instead of two. If the remembered page has been deleted, you're gracefully bounced back to /notebook instead of seeing an endless skeleton.

Faster Event Summary Tracker: AI Weekly Summary no longer blocks the initial render, reviewer names are fetched in parallel on the server, and the page now caches its last good payload in your browser so reloads paint instantly while fresh data refreshes in the background.

Refreshed the Event Summary Tracker stats panel: status counts now appear as a single color-coded progress bar with a submission-rate headline that includes both 'Meet' and 'Didn't Meet' as submitted.
Today page now handles narrow screens better: Big 3 'Done' and clear buttons stack below the card on small widths, and long card/checklist titles wrap onto multiple lines instead of being truncated.
Checklist items in board cards can now be reordered by dragging and dropping. Items stay within their section (ungrouped or a named group) and the new order is saved immediately.
Event Summary Tracker API now fetches snapshots, submissions, occurrences, and sync data in parallel, making weekly views load noticeably faster.
The review modal now shows the circle's actual roster size (from CCB) instead of a computed attended-minus-guests figure.
Fixed a bug in the Event Summary Tracker review modal where notes submitted via the Radius app would disappear after the live data fetch completed.
Renamed the "Rows" stat in the Event Summary Tracker to "Circles" for clearer labeling.
Centered the Mark all reviewed action in the Event Summary Tracker stats card on mobile so the button is easier to find and tap on small screens.

Added a new Event Summary Tracker page (beta). One screen to see, week by week, which Circles submitted an event summary through CCB and which still need attention. Three buckets — Needs Review, Awaiting Submission, and Complete — plus a Mark all reviewed button so you can clear a week of stale summaries in one click. The page also surfaces orphan alerts when CCB has a summary for a Circle that doesn't match your filter or isn't in Radius, and flags Circles that haven't met for 2+ scheduled weeks. AI Weekly Summaries are now saved per user, so Hunter's view and Trip's view don't overwrite each other.
Cleaned up the Circle summary thank-you page by removing the redundant checkmark icon — the Circles logo and headline now stand on their own.

Reworked the Circle Meetings Calendar event-summary flow. Removed the flaky manual Auto-Update button — statuses now sync silently from CCB in the background whenever you open or change weeks (throttled to 30 minutes so it never hammers CCB). Each leader row now shows a Review button when an event summary exists but hasn't been reviewed yet, opening a modal with the submitted summary, did-not-meet, or 'not submitted' state. When CCB disagrees with what's in RADIUS, you get a Review Mismatches panel where you can keep the current value or override with CCB per leader — sync never silently overwrites human input. Every state change is now audited.

Redesigned the Prayer List for a calmer, distraction-free read. Each prayer is now a clean row with no embedded boxes, due dates surface in plain text, and you can log a prayer time with its own note attached to that specific session — your past sessions stay in the history without changing the original request.

Fixed a second auto-update bug: when reading from the cached 8-week CCB attendance payload, the matching logic wasn't filtering by date, so a leader's real submission from any prior week could match and incorrectly mark them "received" for the current week. Auto-update and the cleanup endpoint now pass an explicit week window to the matcher, and any event whose occurrence date falls outside that window is discarded before matching.

Cleaned up the 12 circles that had been incorrectly auto-marked as "received" by the old CCB matching logic. Added a /api/ccb/reset-false-received admin endpoint (CRON_SECRET-gated, dry-run by default) that re-checks every currently-received leader against fresh CCB attendance data and resets any that don't have actual evidence (notes, headcount, or explicit did-not-meet). Safe to re-run at any time.

Tuned the daily CCB sync to only refresh per-group calendars for circles that meet that day (~50-70 groups instead of all 312). Bulk attendance still refreshes for every group via a single global call, so the dashboard Auto-Update Summaries button stays fully current for everyone. A safety net also catches any group whose cache hasn't been touched in 7+ days. Cuts the daily sync runtime by ~75% and leaves even more headroom under CCB's rate limit.

Fixed a long-standing bug where the Auto-Update Summaries button could mark a circle as "received" even when no summary had been submitted. CCB pre-creates an event record for every scheduled occurrence — those empty shells were matching as valid reports. Now a CCB event only counts as a report if there's actual evidence: an explicit "did not meet" flag, notes/topic/prayer entered, or a non-zero headcount. Existing leaders that were already marked "received" in error will need to be reset manually on the dashboard.

Redesigned how RADIUS talks to CCB so we can never bleed the API limit again. Bulk CCB attendance is now pulled once a day (4 AM CT) into Supabase, and both Circle Summary and the dashboard Auto-Update Summaries button read from that cache by default — cache-first, with live CCB only as a fallback. Added a hard circuit breaker in the CCB client (40 calls/min, 500/hr) so even a future code bug can't run away. The dashboard auto-update response now reports `ccb_source: cache | live` so we can see what each click hit. Net effect: ~99% fewer CCB calls per day compared to the broken 10-minute prewarm.

Disabled the every-10-minute Circle Summary CCB prewarm job, which was burning through our CCB API rate limit (~200 requests every 10 minutes across all active groups). The scheduled function is now gated behind an env flag (CIRCLE_SUMMARY_PREWARM_ENABLED) and defaults to off. Circle Summary will fall back to its previous on-demand fetch path until we redesign the warmer to be lazy / scoped / off-peak.

Auto-update from CCB now matches every circle that has a CCB attendance record for the week. Two underlying bugs were fixed: (1) CCB returns occurrence dates as "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss" which Luxon's ISO parser rejected, leaving the occurrence date null and causing valid "did not meet" records to be skipped, and (2) leader-to-event matching now also tries cached CCB event IDs and bidirectional group-name substrings, so circles whose CCB title doesn't contain the leader's exact name still get pulled back. Added a /api/ccb/auto-update-summaries?debug=1 mode that returns per-leader match diagnostics for troubleshooting.

Personalized the Circle Summary header — the title now reads "[Leader's first name]'s Circle" (and "…'s Roster") instead of a generic "Your Circle." Removed the small "Valley Creek Church" label, lifted SIGN OUT into the top corner, and made the title font fluid so longer names stay on a single line.

Circle Summary events now serve from a shared cache that's refreshed every 10 minutes in the background. Leaders no longer wait for CCB on first visit, on cold starts, or after switching devices — the page loads with up-to-date events almost instantly.

Made Circle Summary noticeably faster. Leaders who are already signed in now skip straight to their events page with no flash of the sign-in form, and the events list pulls less frequently from CCB so repeat navigations feel instant. A full-page green splash with the Circle Summary mark now shows during loading transitions instead of a half-rendered page.
Added the Circles logo to the Circle Summary headers and made it the home-screen icon when leaders install Circle Summary as a PWA.

The Refresh button on the Circle Summary events page now hides itself when it's not doing anything. It only appears as a 'Refreshing…' indicator while a background refresh is in flight, then disappears.

Added a Refresh button to the Circle Summary Roster. If someone's phone, email, or birthday changes in CCB, tap Refresh to pull the latest data right away instead of waiting for the daily background sync.

The Circle Summary Roster now loads almost instantly. Member details (phone, email, birthday) are cached on the server and shown immediately from your last visit, then refreshed in the background. The previous one-by-one CCB lookups have been replaced with a single batched request, so even the first load is significantly faster.

Polished the Circle Summary Roster experience: the Events/Roster tab switcher now uses a clear green active state that matches the hero, and the phone Call/Text action sheet was rebuilt with the Circle Summary design language — bold Anton-styled phone number, on-brand action buttons, and proper contrast that no longer washes out behind the dark theme.

Circle Summary now has a Roster tab. Leaders can view everyone on their Circle, tap a phone number to choose Call or Text, tap an email to send a message, and see upcoming birthdays. A friendly banner highlights any birthdays in the next 14 days (dismissable per-person until the birthday passes). Add/remove uses the same flow as the events form.
RADIUS now lives at vccradius.netlify.app. Magic links, email links, and PWA install instructions all point to the new home. Email is still sent from noreply@myradiuscrm.com.

Restored the Circle Summary Page admin button so it auto-signs the leader in again. The magic-link route now verifies the fresh token first and only falls back to an existing session when the token is missing or expired (the bookmark case).

Adding the Circle Summary to your home screen now opens directly into Circle Summary instead of the main RADIUS login. The Circle Summary now ships its own web-app manifest so iOS treats it as a standalone leader app.

Bookmarking your Circle Summary magic link now keeps you signed in. The link route checks for an active leader session before re-verifying the token, so the bookmark works even after the 7-day token has expired.

Polished the Update Roster UI on the Circle Summary — clearer Edit/Done toggle with icons, softer warning banner, restyled remove buttons with hover states, and cleaner alignment for newly added attendees.
Redesigned Help Center — fuzzy search, category chips, expandable knowledge-base articles, real-world workflows, glossary, FAQ, and a What's New strip pulled from the update log.
Find Leaders search now shows only Active leaders, hiding invited, on-boarding, paused, off-boarding, and archived statuses.
Roster column on Find Leaders is now hidden for signed-out visitors and only shown to authenticated users.
Circle Summary now shows CCB notes consistently even when a local draft exists for that event; CCB notes are returned as dedicated reference notes instead of being hidden by draft source handling.
Circle Summary now also checks CCB for the composed previous notes when a submitted RADIUS summary has its answer stored in dynamic responses instead of the legacy notes field.
The "Notes from your last summary" card on Circle Summary now has an "Edit these notes" button that drops the previous notes into the gathering textarea so you can update them in place.
Clearer wording on the "previously saved notes" reference card in Circle Summary — now explains the notes are just for reference and don't need to be retyped.
Circle Summary now reliably pulls in notes and attendees that were entered directly in CCB — an empty auto-saved draft from a previous visit no longer hides the prefilled data.
Notes fields on the Circle Summary form now auto-expand as you type, so longer reflections are easy to read without scrolling inside a tiny box.
Message Center announcements now only appear on the Circle Summary events list — they no longer clutter the individual summary submission page.
Renamed the third step of the Circle Summary form from "A few more things" to "Tell us more" — a friendlier prompt for sharing notes about the gathering.
The "Circle Summary Page" button on a Circle Leader profile now auto-signs you in as that leader and opens their events page in a new tab — no more sign-in screen for admins.

Circle Summary events list is now bulletproof on accuracy: after you submit a summary, the list force-refreshes from CCB instead of showing a cached "Pending" badge. Added a Refresh button in the header with a live "Refreshing…" indicator, automatic refresh when you switch back to the tab, and a shorter server-side cache so CCB-direct edits surface within seconds.
Moved Dynamic Questions admin into the Tools menu and renamed it to "Circle Summary Questions" for easier access. The standalone Circle Summary tab in Organization Settings has been removed.

New Message Center on the Circle Summary events page: admins can post rich-text announcements (with optional link button) that appear as a banner below the green hero. Manage messages under the new "Message Center" tab on /admin/dynamic-questions — supports campus targeting, start/end dates, and priority ordering.

Added a "View leader's Circle Summary page" link in the Event Summary section of each Circle Leader profile, so you can open the leader-facing summary page directly in a new tab (requires CCB Group ID).

Board keyboard shortcuts now work while a card is open: 0–9 toggle labels, C completes, M assigns/unassigns you, F toggles focus, Del/Backspace deletes (with confirm). Typing inside the title, description, comments, or checklist inputs still behaves normally.
Board cards with a linked Circle Leader now show "View CCB Calendar" and "Edit Group in CCB" buttons in the card detail panel (when the leader has a CCB Group ID set).

Circle profile → CCB Circle Link section now shows two buttons when a CCB Group ID is set: "View CCB Calendar" (jumps to the group's events) and "Edit Group in CCB" (opens the group edit page in CCB).

Circle Summary reminder emails: follow-up emails now use warm, inviting language ("we'd love to hear about your Circle") instead of deficit framing. Magic link now lands on the events list page so leaders always land on a working screen. Footer now says "staff contact" instead of referencing ACPDs.

Circle Summary → CCB: when a leader adds new people via "Add to roster", those pending names are now also counted as guests on the CCB event summary. So 7 checked roster attendees + 2 pending adds shows as 9 total attendance in CCB (7 attendees + 2 guests).

Circle Summary events page: cached events list now persists across browser sessions so repeat visits paint instantly, and the loading state shows "Loading your Circle events…" — escalating to "Still loading — pulling the latest from Church Community Builder" after 5 seconds so leaders know the page isn't stuck.

Circle Summary → CCB: the notes pushed to CCB now preserve the line breaks leaders type (paragraph breaks, lists) instead of being flattened into one line. Dynamic question answers each appear on their own block with the question as a heading; the "Additional questions —" prefix is gone.

Circle Summary: "Add someone to my Circle" requests are now appended to the bottom of the notes pushed to CCB (one blank line before each block, format: "Add New Person to Roster:" then name, phone, email on their own lines) — making it easy for ACPDs to spot and process them. The pending entries no longer go through a separate roster-request flow.

Circle Summary attendance: pending "add to roster" requests now have always-visible Edit and Remove actions on each row, so leaders can fix a typo or remove someone they added by mistake before submitting.

Circle Summary → CCB push: literal \n\n characters no longer appear in Event Notes when the leader includes line breaks in dynamic question responses, the topic, prayer requests, or info fields. All text headed to CCB is now flattened to a single line with " || " between paragraph breaks.
Settings → Circle Summary: removed the Submission Log and Info Update Requests tiles. Those admin pages have been retired.

Circle Summary attendance: roster removal is now behind an Edit toggle — tap Edit to reveal a red minus on each removable person, with a clear instruction line warning that the action removes them from the Circle's roster. Tap Done to return to taking attendance.
Circle Summary Thank You page now uses leader-friendly language ("Your Circle summary has been submitted.") and no longer shows an internal reference code.
Circle Summary sign-in now asks for email only — phone number is no longer offered as an identifier, simplifying the leader login flow.
Circle Summary: removed the "VC" emblem from the sign-in hero for a cleaner header.
Circle Summary: the SMS sent from the admin magic-link tool now reads as friendly end-user copy — "here's your link to report your Circle event summary" — instead of the technical sign-in phrasing.
Boards: hover a card and press F to toggle its focus state — same as clicking 'Set as Focus' in the card detail, but without opening the card.

Circle Summary loads noticeably faster: the events page paints the header instantly while CCB data loads in the background, CCB results are cached for 60 seconds, and the events list is remembered so navigating back from an event is near-instant. The sign-in page no longer flashes for already-signed-in leaders.
Fixed odd characters (literal \n\n) appearing in CCB Event Notes when a Circle Summary included line breaks. Paragraph breaks are now displayed cleanly as " || " separators.
Circle Summary form now shows only dynamic questions with no extra static fields. Inputs and radio buttons have a more visible border for easier tapping and typing.

Circle Leaders can now remove someone from their roster on the Circle Summary attendance page. The Remove action only takes the person out of the Circle's CCB group — their CCB profile is not changed.
Circle Summary admin pages (Dynamic Questions, Submission Log, Info Update Requests) are now linked from a dedicated Circle Summary tab in Settings.

Circle Summary form now shows only the questions configured in Dynamic Questions — no default fields. The admin page shows a warning when no active questions are configured, status badges (Always / Active / Upcoming / Expired) on each question, and a clear Visibility toggle when creating or editing a question.

Circle Summary magic links now reliably sign leaders in. The auth handler sets the session cookie on a 200 page and then navigates, avoiding a hosting quirk that was dropping the cookie on the redirect and bouncing leaders back to the sign-in screen.
Choosing a primary leader from CCB on Circle forms now also fills in that person's CCB profile link automatically, so you don't have to copy the individual URL by hand.

Admin "send magic link" now builds the link using the host you're currently on, so leaders aren't bounced through a cross-domain redirect that strips their session cookie. Tapping the link signs them straight into Circle Summary as intended.

"Send magic link" on a Circle page now shows a clear error if the request fails (instead of crashing with "Unexpected end of JSON input"), and the server returns the actual error message instead of a bare 500.
Circle Summary reflection step is now a single open-ended prompt — "Tell us about your Circle" — instead of four separate fields. Less friction, more room for whatever the leader wants to share.

Circle Summary email reminders: turn on per-leader from the Quick Actions panel and they'll get a VCC-branded, mobile-friendly email an hour before each meeting plus a follow-up every morning until they submit. Each email has a one-tap link that signs them in automatically — no password.

On a Circle Leader profile, the Event Summary panel now has a "Text Circle Summary link" button. Tap it and your phone's texting app opens with a pre-filled message containing a magic sign-in link addressed to the leader — perfect for nudging a leader from your phone after Circle.

Circle Summary URLs now include the leader's CCB group ID (e.g. /circle-leader-toolkit/12345/events) so you can tell which Circle you're looking at. The events list and submission form always show the logged-in leader's own Circle — manually changing the group ID in the URL redirects you back to your own Circle, and all data still comes from the session, so other Circles' data can't be reached.

Auto-Update from CCB no longer marks circles as "Did Not Meet" if their meeting is scheduled for today or later in the week. CCB pre-creates attendance records before meetings happen and defaults them to Did Not Meet — the auto-update now skips those until the meeting date has passed.

Circle Summary is live: leaders sign in with a 6-digit code emailed to them, see their last 8 weeks of Circle meetings, and submit topic, notes, prayer requests, attendees, and follow-up items in one tap. Submissions push straight into CCB and email the ACPD automatically. Editing a previously submitted summary pre-fills the form so corrections don't need to be retyped.

ACPD admin pages for Circle Summary: build dynamic questions that appear on the leader form (with date windows and field types), review meeting-info change requests with one click to apply them to the leader's profile, and audit every submission with CCB push status.
Circle Summary 'Did your Circle meet?' toggle now reliably renders as a green pill switch when on, instead of falling back to a default checkbox in some browsers.

Notebook pages now support Ink mode: flip an individual page from rich text to a drawing surface, write with Apple Pencil or any pointer device, pinch or wheel-zoom, erase whole strokes, and keep text and ink saved independently.

Notebook feels dramatically faster: pages you've already visited open instantly from an in-memory cache, switching pages no longer triggers a blank spinner, the root /notebook redirect no longer double-fetches your folders, the heavy rich-text editor and link pickers now load on-demand, and every loading state shows a content-shaped skeleton instead of a lonely spinner.

Notebook page editor reworked on iPad — the title is now an auto-growing textarea (no more text getting scrolled or clipped inside an input element), padding from the sidebar scales generously across tablet breakpoints (56px on iPad portrait, 107px on landscape), the title font scales up at md and uses the page font family, and the title's bottom margin is restored so it visually separates from the body.

Notebook on iPad now adapts to orientation — in portrait the right-side links panel tucks away as a bottom sheet so the typing area gets full width, and rotating to landscape docks the panel back alongside the editor.

Auto-update from CCB now matches leaders even when CCB titles include a co-leader name (e.g. "FMT | S2 | Shannon and Sherrie Hawk") by checking that both the leader's first and last name appear in the event title — a huge bump in how many summaries get pulled in a single click.

Auto-update from CCB now falls back to matching by the leader's name when their stored CCB group name doesn't quite match the event title in CCB — so leaders whose group name in RADIUS includes extra detail finally get their summaries pulled.

The CCB Event Explorer no longer silently shows "No events found" when CCB is actually rejecting the request — credential and service-permission errors from CCB now surface with the real error message so problems can be diagnosed instead of guessed at.
The floating quick-actions menu is simpler now — the extra Start a New Note shortcut has been removed so the list stays focused on the actions you actually use in the moment.
Prayer points on the Prayer page now keep separate Pray on and Prayed on dates, so you can schedule when to pray and also log when you actually prayed without overwriting either one.
Notebook folder icons now use a categorized Lucide icon picker instead of emoji, with selected icons shown directly in the sidebar for a cleaner, more consistent UI.
Checklist suggestions now let you choose whether to add items to an existing checklist or start a new one — no more duplicate checklists.
AI Summarize now inserts properly formatted content — headers, bullet lists, and line breaks are preserved as rich text instead of collapsing into a wall of plain text.
Board cards now have AI checklist suggestions — tap "Suggest" in the Checklists section to get AI-generated next steps and open items pulled from the card's description.
Board card descriptions now support voice dictation and AI summarize — tap the mic to speak your notes directly into the description field, or use AI Summarize to clean them up.
URLs in description fields (boards, notes) are now automatically rendered as clickable links — no need to manually insert a link with the toolbar.

Paste Import on the Bulk Message page — paste a raw First / Last / Phone list, review and edit parsed entries inline, then add them to your recipient list in one click. Pasted entries are saved with the rest of your list using the existing "Save as List" flow.

New Copy for CCB shortcut on the Notes page — click the clipboard icon on any note to copy it as plain text and open the leader's CCB profile in one click, ready to paste. Or use "Save + Copy for CCB" while composing to save and copy in one step.
Notebook sidebar now updates immediately when pages are deleted or changed, so removed pages disappear right away and newly available pages reliably show up without needing a refresh.

Notebook AI checklists: use Suggest in the Checklists panel to read the current note and propose concise next-step or outstanding-item checklist entries, with source context shown underneath each item.
Notebook-linked cards now support AI checklist suggestions too — open a linked card from the Notebook page, use Suggest in the card checklist area, and add selected items directly to the card.

Saved checklist items now use a cleaner two-line layout: a short item title with smaller supporting context beneath it. AI-created checklist items preserve their source note line as the supporting detail.
Creating a card from a Notebook page now defaults the card title to the note title, keeps it editable before saving, and adds a backlink to the original note in the card description.
New Notebooks page: organize your notes in one place with folders, page navigation, and a focused writing workspace for ongoing leader conversations and follow-up planning.
Added subtle left padding to the Notebook page title field so title text starts with better spacing and aligns more cleanly with editor content.
Cleaned up Notebook checklist controls by moving bulk checklist actions into a compact actions menu, reducing visual clutter while keeping Mark all complete, Clear completed, and Duplicate available.
Refined Notebook checklist readability by adding left padding inside checklist title and item text fields and reducing checklist text size for a cleaner, less crowded look.

Notebook checklists now default to "Checklist" when you create one, long checklist item text wraps fully, and each checklist includes quick bulk actions to Mark all complete, Clear completed, or Duplicate.
Fixed Add Leader reliability and cleaned up duplicate Status options in the Add Leader form so each status now appears once and leader creation uses the secured API flow.

Notebook pages can now be moved and reordered by dragging in the sidebar — hover a page to reveal the drag handle, then drag within a folder or into a different folder. Order and folder placement are saved automatically.
Mass Update now supports Status — you can bulk-change the status (invited, on-boarding, active, paused, off-boarding) for multiple circle leaders at once from the Import & Manage Circles page.

Host Team Leaders — Track volunteer team leaders (ushers, kids, hospitality, etc.) alongside Circle Leaders using the same relationship tools. Add Host Team Leaders via the Add Leader form, filter by type on the Find Leaders page, and manage HT Directors in Settings.

Unified button and color styling across the entire app — all pages and modals now use the same consistent design system instead of a mix of blue, green, purple, and teal variants. The Bulk Message iMessage bubble and START BATCH button are intentionally kept in iMessage blue.
Fixed a touch dead zone on the Events calendar — the Quick Actions FAB was invisibly blocking taps on the Summary, Reminder, Profile, and CCB buttons when the FAB was closed.
Backend security hardening: moved CCB API credentials out of source code into environment variables, and added admin-only protection to the campus creation endpoint.
Performance improvements across the app — reduced unnecessary re-renders in the auth layer, sped up board data loading, and reduced the amount of data fetched when loading circle leader notes.
When turning on a follow-up from the circle profile, the generated Notes timeline entry now includes the full follow-up message you typed (not just the date).
Circle follow-up quick action now switches labels based on status: Turn On when follow-up is off, and Turn Off when it's active.
Follow-up and profile updates now consistently log to the circle leader Notes timeline when you save edits, including follow-up note/date changes.
Circle follow-up quick actions now use clearer labels: the top action says Turn Off and the date-row action says Edit.
Confirmation dialogs now center and evenly spread action buttons across the modal footer for clearer button layout on follow-up and other confirm prompts.
Circle Quick Actions follow-up now shows an Edit button (instead of the Required badge), and the follow-up modal lets you update both date and note in one place.
Circle follow-up cards were cleaned up so the Change Date action sits near the date at the top, and the temporary 'Due Soon' status message was removed for a simpler quick actions layout.
Circle profile follow-ups now show the saved follow-up note in Quick Actions, and follow-up notes added from the Quick Actions + menu are automatically written to the leader's Notes timeline.
Boards: Click any column's color dot to change it from a 12-color palette. New columns also automatically get a unique color so no two columns look the same.
Boards: The 'Add Column' button now matches the Tools button style in the nav — consistent frosted glass look across the app.
When importing circle leaders to a board, the auto-generated card description now puts each detail (campus, circle type, day, time, phone, email) on its own line for easier reading.
Board descriptions now show tighter contact chips, and matching call, text, and email actions also appear on board comments and circle notes when contact info is mentioned.
Board card descriptions now surface phone numbers and email addresses as one-tap contact actions so you can call, text, or email directly from the card.

Circle leader pages now show a birthday banner when a leader's (or additional leader's) birthday is within the next 7 days — highlighted in violet alongside the existing prayer and encouragement banners.
The Add Board Card modal now remembers the last board and list you chose, so you don't have to re-select them every time. Cards also support an optional description field for adding context or notes.
Prayer requests can now have a scheduled date — they'll appear on your Today page and in the daily digest email on the day you want to pray for them.
Boards buttons (New Board, view toggle) now use the same subtle style as the Tools nav button — consistent look across the app.
The Tools button in the top nav now shows a label and dropdown arrow — no more mystery icon.
Quick Actions FAB: Tap the + button in the bottom-left corner to instantly log a note, connection, or prayer, set a follow-up, or add a board card — from anywhere in the app.
Boards: You can now sort your boards by Newest, A–Z, Last Updated, or a Custom drag-and-drop order. Your preference is saved automatically.
Prayer Page: Leader names are now clickable links that take you directly to their profile.
Added Update Log to the account menu (below Help) so you can always see what's changed in RADIUS.
Bulk Message: You can now search for and manually add individual leaders to a message batch — even ones that don't match your current filters. Search by name, phone, or campus and tap to add.
Bulk Message: Added keyboard shortcuts during a batch — press Space or Enter to open Messages, press S to skip. Shortcut hints are shown on the buttons.
Bulk Message: You can now pause a batch mid-way and resume right where you left off instead of starting over.
